title: GO GO! Comic Strip Website Frontend Implementation

requirements.summary: Create comic book themed homepage with bold 'GO GO!' branding and consistent styling across all pages
acceptanceCriteria: The site header displays 'GO GO!' in a large, comic-styled font
The color scheme and typography reflect a comic book / pop-art aesthetic
The layout uses a comic-panel style grid for content areas
The style is consistent across all pages

file_operations.description: Add comic book aesthetic global styles including bold outlines, halftone texture patterns, pop-art color palette (red, yellow, black), comic-panel grid utilities, and punchy typography with custom font imports. Include CSS custom properties for theme colors and reusable halftone/action-line patterns.

Extend Tailwind theme with comic book color palette (red, yellow, black variations), custom font families for comic-style headings, box-shadow utilities for bold outlines, and spacing for comic-panel grid layouts.

Create a Layout component with header displaying the 'GO GO!' logo using the generated image at frontend/public/assets/generated/gogo-logo.dim_600x200.png, hero banner section using frontend/public/assets/generated/hero-banner.dim_1200x400.png, and consistent comic-themed styling. This component wraps all pages to ensure style consistency.
Wrap all routes with the Layout component to apply consistent comic book styling and 'GO GO!' branding across all pages. Ensure the Layout is the top-level wrapper inside the Router.

requirements.summary: Display PG-13 content disclaimer splash screen requiring user acknowledgment before site access
acceptanceCriteria: A PG-13 disclaimer is shown when the user first visits the site
The disclaimer explains the content is intended for teens and adults
The user must click an 'I Understand' or 'Enter' button to proceed
The disclaimer does not re-appear during the same session after acknowledgment

file_operations.description: Create a DisclaimerSplash component displaying a PG-13 content warning in a comic-styled modal overlay. Include message text explaining content is suitable for teens and adults (PG-13), an 'I Understand' or 'Enter' button to proceed, and comic book styling with bold outlines and pop-art colors.

Create a custom hook to manage disclaimer acknowledgment state using sessionStorage. Track whether the user has acknowledged the disclaimer in the current session and provide functions to check and set acknowledgment status.

Integrate DisclaimerSplash component using the useDisclaimer hook. Show the disclaimer on initial load if not acknowledged; hide all other content until the user clicks 'I Understand'. Ensure disclaimer does not re-appear during the same session.

requirements.summary: Create comic strip catalog page displaying all comics as styled cards with 'Legends of the Hidden Butt' listed first
acceptanceCriteria: A catalog page displays comic strips as individual cards
'Legends of the Hidden Butt' appears as the first card
Each card has a visible title and a 'Read' button
The cards are styled consistently with the comic book theme

file_operations.description: Create CatalogPage component that fetches comic strips from the backend using the getComicStrips() method. Display comics as a grid of comic-styled cards. Each card shows the comic title, cover art (use frontend/public/assets/generated/comic-cover-legends.dim_400x300.png for 'Legends of the Hidden Butt'), and a 'Read' button linking to the reader page. Apply comic book styling with bold outlines and pop-art colors.

Create ComicCard component to render individual comic strip cards. Display title, cover image, and 'Read' button. Style with bold comic-panel borders, halftone patterns, and pop-art colors consistent with the site theme. Accept comic data (title, slideId, cover image path) as props.
Add route for the catalog page at '/catalog' path. Register CatalogPage component with the router and ensure it is wrapped by the Layout component for consistent styling.

requirements.summary: Create comic reader page embedding Google Slides presentation with navigation back to catalog
acceptanceCriteria: The reader page embeds the Google Slides presentation in a full-width iframe
The embed URL used is the published/embed format for the provided presentation ID
The comic title 'Legends of the Hidden Butt' is displayed above the embed
A 'Back to Library' navigation link is present
The reader page is accessible from the catalog card's 'Read' button

file_operations.description: Create ReaderPage component that accepts a comic slideId as a URL parameter. Display the comic title at the top, embed the Google Slides presentation using an iframe with the URL format https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/{slideId}/embed, and include a 'Back to Library' link navigating to the catalog page. Style the page with comic book theme elements and ensure the iframe is responsive and full-width.

Add route for the reader page at '/reader/:slideId' path. Register ReaderPage component with the router, passing slideId as a parameter. Ensure the ComicCard 'Read' button links to this route with the correct slideId.
